name: iprogress

metadata.name: IProgress
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Library or Lesser General Public License (LGPL)
metadata.summary: Text progress bar library for Python.

documentation:
Text progress bar library for Python.

A text progress bar is typically used to display the progress of a long
running operation, providing a visual cue that processing is underway.

The ProgressBar class manages the current progress, and the format of the line
is given by a number of widgets. A widget is an object that may display
differently depending on the state of the progress bar. There are three types
of widgets:
 - a string, which always shows itself

 - a ProgressBarWidget, which may return a different value every time its
   update method is called

 - a ProgressBarWidgetHFill, which is like ProgressBarWidget, except it
   expands to fill the remaining width of the line.

The progressbar module is very easy to use, yet very powerful. It will also
automatically enable features like auto-resizing when the system supports it.

classes.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar

methods.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar.__init__
documentation: Initializes a progress bar with sane defaults.

methods.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar.__call__
documentation: Use a ProgressBar to iterate through an iterable.

methods.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar._env_size
documentation: Tries to find the term_width from the environment.

methods.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar._handle_resize
documentation: Tries to catch resize signals sent from the terminal.

methods.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar.percentage
documentation: Returns the progress as a percentage.

methods.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar._format_line
documentation: Joins the widgets and justifies the line.

methods.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar._need_update
documentation: Returns whether the ProgressBar should redraw the line.

methods.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar._update_widgets
documentation: Checks all widgets for the time sensitive bit.

methods.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar.update
documentation: Updates the ProgressBar to a new value.

methods.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar.start
documentation:
Starts measuring time, and prints the bar at 0%.

It returns self so you can use it like this:
\>>> pbar = ProgressBar().start()
\>>> for i in range(100):
... # do something
... pbar.update(i+1)
...
\>>> pbar.finish()

methods.name: IProgress.progressbar.ProgressBar.finish
documentation: Puts the ProgressBar bar in the finished state.

The ProgressBar class which updates and prints the bar.

A common way of using it is like:
\>>> pbar = ProgressBar().start()
\>>> for i in range(100):
... # do something
... pbar.update(i+1)
...
\>>> pbar.finish()

You can also use a ProgressBar as an iterator:
\>>> progress = ProgressBar()
\>>> for i in progress(some_iterable):
... # do something
...

Since the progress bar is incredibly customizable you can specify
different widgets of any type in any order. You can even write your own
widgets! However, since there are already a good number of widgets you
should probably play around with them before moving on to create your own
widgets.

The term_width parameter represents the current terminal width. If the
parameter is set to an integer then the progress bar will use that,
otherwise it will attempt to determine the terminal width falling back to
80 columns if the width cannot be determined.

When implementing a widget's update method you are passed a reference to
the current progress bar. As a result, you have access to the
ProgressBar's methods and attributes. Although there is nothing preventing
you from changing the ProgressBar you should treat it as read only.

Useful methods and attributes include (Public API):
: - currval: current progress (0 \<= currval \<= maxval)
  - maxval: maximum (and final) value
  - finished: True if the bar has finished (reached 100%)
  - start_time: the time when start() method of ProgressBar was called
  - seconds_elapsed: seconds elapsed since start_time and last call to
    : update
  - percentage(): progress in percent [0..100]

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.Widget

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.Widget.update
documentation:
Updates the widget.

pbar - a reference to the calling ProgressBar

The base class for all widgets.

The ProgressBar will call the widget's update value when the widget should
be updated. The widget's size may change between calls, but the widget may
display incorrectly if the size changes drastically and repeatedly.

The boolean TIME_SENSITIVE informs the ProgressBar that it should be
updated more often because it is time sensitive.

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.WidgetHFill

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.WidgetHFill.update
documentation:
Updates the widget providing the total width the widget must fill.

pbar - a reference to the calling ProgressBar
width - The total width the widget must fill

The base class for all variable width widgets.

This widget is much like the \hfill command in TeX, it will expand to
fill the line. You can use more than one in the same line, and they will
all have the same width, and together will fill the line.

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.Timer

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.Timer.format_time
documentation: Formats time as the string "HH:MM:SS".

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.Timer.update
documentation: Updates the widget to show the elapsed time.
Widget which displays the elapsed seconds.

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.ETA

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.ETA.update
documentation: Updates the widget to show the ETA or total time when finished.
Widget which attempts to estimate the time of arrival.

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.AdaptiveETA

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.AdaptiveETA.update
documentation: Updates the widget to show the ETA or total time when finished.

Widget which attempts to estimate the time of arrival.

Uses a weighted average of two estimates:
  1) ETA based on the total progress and time elapsed so far
  2) ETA based on the progress as per tha last 10 update reports

The weight depends on the current progress so that to begin with the
total progress is used and at the end only the most recent progress is
used.

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.FileTransferSpeed

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.FileTransferSpeed.update
documentation: Updates the widget with the current SI prefixed speed.
Widget for showing the transfer speed (useful for file transfers).

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.AnimatedMarker

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.AnimatedMarker.update
documentation:
Updates the widget to show the next marker or the first marker when
finished

An animated marker for the progress bar which defaults to appear as if
it were rotating.

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.Counter
documentation: Displays the current count.

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.Percentage
documentation: Displays the current percentage as a number with a percent sign.

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.FormatLabel
documentation: Displays a formatted label.

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.SimpleProgress
documentation: Returns progress as a count of the total (e.g.: "5 of 47").

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.Bar

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.Bar.__init__
documentation:
Creates a customizable progress bar.

marker - string or updatable object to use as a marker
left - string or updatable object to use as a left border
right - string or updatable object to use as a right border
fill - character to use for the empty part of the progress bar
fill_left - whether to fill from the left or the right

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.Bar.update
documentation: Updates the progress bar and its subcomponents.
A progress bar which stretches to fill the line.

classes.name: IProgress.widgets.ReverseBar

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.ReverseBar.__init__
documentation:
Creates a customizable progress bar.

marker - string or updatable object to use as a marker
left - string or updatable object to use as a left border
right - string or updatable object to use as a right border
fill - character to use for the empty part of the progress bar
fill_left - whether to fill from the left or the right
A bar which has a marker which bounces from side to side.

methods.name: IProgress.widgets.BouncingBar.update
documentation: Updates the progress bar and its subcomponents.
